title: libpod: inhibit SIGTERM during cleanup()
body:
The network cleanup can handle it when it is killed half way through as it spits out a bunch of error in that case on the next cleanup attempt. Try to avoid getting into such a state and ignore sigterm during this section.

Of course we stil can get SIGKILL so we should work on fixing the underlying problems in network cleanup but let's see if this helps us with the CI flakes in the meantime.

We were not able to find or create Copr project `packit/containers-podman-23460` specified in the config with the following error:
```
Packit received HTTP 500 Internal Server Error from Copr Service. Check the Copr status page: https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/status/stats/, or ask for help in Fedora Build System matrix channel https://matrix.to/#/#buildsys:fedoraproject.org.
```
---
Unless the HTTP status code above is >= 500,  please check your configuration for:

1. typos in owner and project name (groups need to be prefixed with `@`)
2. whether the project name doesn't contain not allowed characters (only letters, digits, underscores, dashes and dots must be used)
3. whether the project itself exists (Packit creates projects only in its own namespace)
4. whether Packit is allowed to build in your Copr project
5. whether your Copr project/group is not private

@edsantiago please pick this into your no retry PR and let me know if this seems to do anything.
If it doesn't help there is no point in merging this.

[Does not seem to help](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5817423608152064/html/int-podman-fedora-39-rootless-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-kube-play-test-HostAliases--1). Or at least it does not resolve the bug entirely.

I think the fix is good to have, but when pods are involved we cleanup the cgroups causing a SIGKILL for each conmon+podman cleanup process, and that can explain why the failure is happening in a kube test.

> I think the fix is good to have, but when pods are involved we cleanup the cgroups causing a SIGKILL for each conmon+podman cleanup process, and that can explain why the failure is happening in a kube test.

yes I noticed this but systemd seems to be sending SIGTERM first and I fixed the pod cleanup issue there with https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23457 for the pod exit policy stop. Not sure what other places this happens. Clearly there are more given the flake persists and we even see this without pods

LGTM

Worth getting this in, even if it does not fully fix the problem.
LGTM on my end as well... It's worth avoiding SIGTERM during critical moments like cleanup.
